control._vel_gains_domain: duty [-1,1] (NezhaMotor MotorVelocityPid); NOT the old PWM-percent scale
control._sprint_114_note: heading_source/heading_dwell_tol_deg/heading_dwell_rate_dps/heading_lead_bias/plan_lead/terminal_lead/min_speed/actuation_lag/distance_kp/distance_tol/model_tau_lin/model_tau_ang/a_max/a_decel/v_body_max/j_max/yaw_jerk_max added 2026-07-20 (sprint 114 ticket 002, config-as-truth completion): gen_boot_config.py no longer carries these as Python-side *_DEFAULT fallback constants -- every value below is seeded with the exact former default, value-preserving (verified byte-identical boot_config.cpp regen). This profile's own heading_kp/heading_kd (6.0/0.0) and yaw_rate_max/max_rot_accel_dps2 (70/600) predate this note and are unaffected.
control._vel_kff_note: 1/650 -- from duty-saturated plateau measurements 2026-07-11 (620-740 mm/s across windows; window-to-window variance cause not established -- NOT power, per stakeholder). Do not lean on FF accuracy: the loop should be feedback-dominant (see motion_control.ipynb verdict).
control._vel_ki_note: kept 0.0018 in the 2026-07-11 session; motion_control.ipynb then showed the loop is FF-dominated with 40-50% step overshoot and weak steady-state correction -- a feedback-dominant retune (kp/ki up, acceptance via motion_control.ipynb) is the open next step.
control._vel_gains_note: 2026-07-15 RESONANCE TAMED (106-002, live config() gain sweep -- P4 binary wire, tests/bench/velocity_step_response.py, no reflash between trials): vel_kp 0.0014->0.0016, vel_kff 0.00135->0.0008. History: the ORIGINAL 0.0018 loop had a ~140 mm/s overshoot RESONANCE (+24..+33% on a velocity step); the 2026-07-12 interim detune (vel_kp 0.0018->0.0014, vel_ki 0.008->0.005, vel_kaw 15->20) only halved it (~+11%, still over the <~10% bar) and cost rise time. This session's own bench grid at kp=0.0014/kff=0.00135 measured worst-case +25% at 140mm/s (reproducing the historical pattern -- the interim detune alone did not clear the bar this session either). Root cause, found by exhausting kp/ki/kff/iMax/kaw per architecture-update.md (106) Decision 4: kff (feedforward) was OVER-estimated -- raising it (tried 0.0016) made overshoot WORSE (kff*target is an immediate open-loop kick added on top of kp*error, so an over-large kff over-drives the plant right at the step) -- confirming the 2026-07-11 kff derivation's own flagged 620-740mm/s measurement variance was real drift, not noise. Lowering kff to 0.0008 (with kp still at 0.0014) killed the resonance (worst 2.1%) but slowed rise time by ~30-50% (0.9-1.5s vs the interim's 0.9-1.28s) -- kp=0.0016 restores it: 3 consecutive full-grid confirmation runs all passed the <~10% bar with FASTER rise times than the interim detune (worst overshoot 9.3%/4.3%/5.0%; rise times 0.28-0.98s vs the interim's 0.9-1.5s) -- both overshoot AND rise time improved over the shipped interim, not just traded off. vel_ki/vel_kaw unchanged (0.005/20.0) -- the fix lived entirely in kp/kff, ki/kaw were not the lever needed this time. Re-verified from a clean reflash (boot defaults only, no live config() call) after persisting -- see 106-002's own completion notes for the full before/after table. Endpoint-accuracy re-verification (turn_sweep.py-style angle grid) NOT re-run this session -- flagged as a follow-up if endpoint regressions are observed, per the interim detune's own precedent of trading a little endpoint accuracy for trajectory smoothness. vel_filt=0.3 unchanged. See clasi/sprints/106-host-trajectory-planner-profiled-twists-straights-and-turns/issues/heading-loop-output-clamp-and-velocity-resonance.md's own Part 2 (marked resolved by 106-002) for the fuller history.
control._vel_filt_note: kept 0.3: raising to 0.6 (2026-07-11) cut measurement lag but pushed step overshoot to ~11% and turns to +6/+9/+7 -- the decel-lag surplus just became an overshoot surplus. 0.3 is the verified optimum of that trade with the current gains.
control._heading_gains_note: 2026-07-12 BENCH-TUNED (sprint 098-003, playfield over radio): heading_kp = 6.0, heading_kd = 0.0 (pure P). Full angle x speed grid (30/90/180/360 deg x 70..384 mm/s, both directions) lands 100% within +/-1 deg, max |error| 0.84 deg, 90-deg full-ceiling ridge eliminated (was +8..+10 deg open-loop). heading_kp started at 3.0 (98-001) which left a residual ~1-2 deg full-ceiling UNDERSHOOT: a proportional-control steady-state error against terminal motor stiction (near target the P command falls below the wheel deadband and stalls short); kp=6.0 overcomes stiction closer in. Zero commanded terminal reversal (no encoder-wedge trigger). Per-robot tunable: a future integral term would null the last <1 deg entirely -- see clasi/issues/heading-loop-cascade-control-turns-terminate-on-target.md. Re-verify with tests/bench/turn_sweep.py --relay --both.
control._drive_limits_note: 2026-07-12 (sprint 100-001, motion-stack-v2 M1): starting values, not yet bench-tuned, for the new source/drive/ subsystem's Drive::Limits/tracker/policy tunables (PlannerConfig fields 15-31). v_wheel_max=620.0 is the one BENCH-MEASURED exception (mm/s plateau, matching the 2026-07-11 duty-saturated measurements already cited by _vel_kff_note -- NOT a starting guess). track_k_s/track_k_theta/track_k_cross/trim_v_max carry the issue's own Kanayama trim-law table verbatim (2.0/6.0/1.5e-5/120.0 -- track_k_theta=6.0 additionally matches heading_kp's own sprint-098 hardware-proven value, since both are the same physical outer-loop gain generalized). trim_omega_max=2.0 is the issue table's PIVOT-mode value ('1.0 arc / 2.0 pivot [rad/s]'); the lower arc-mode ceiling is left to the tracker's own per-mode logic (ticket 004), not a second wire field -- flagged. replan_err_pos=40.0/handoff_tol_pos=40.0 approximate BOTH the along-track envelope's flat term (40mm) and the cross-track bound (35mm/30mm respectively) with one shared value each -- flagged. handoff_tol_v=0.14 is the issue's flying-handoff formula's own velocity-coupling slope ('e_along <= 0.14*vExit + 40mm'), dimensionally a time constant despite the '_v' name -- flagged. steer_headroom=20.0/wheel_step_max=150.0 have no issue-table anchor at all; picked conservative placeholders (steer_headroom mirrors the old RobotConfig.steerHeadroom starting value; wheel_step_max is ~1.25x trim_v_max) -- flagged. replan_hold=0.2/replan_min_period=0.3/replan_max=3.0/replan_err_theta=0.15/arrive_vel_tol=15.0/arrive_dwell=0.15 are direct transcriptions of the issue's replan-envelope/terminal tables. See ticket 001's completion notes for the full field-by-field derivation; M11 (bench: arc_sweep grids) re-tunes all of these against the real plant.
